concept.title: Two safe strategies for HTML5 customization
concept.shortdesc: There are two reliable approaches to customizing DITA-OT HTML5 output: a plug-in with XSLT import overrides (recommended) or build-time custom XSL via args.xsl.

p.b: Strategy A (recommended): DITA-OT plug-in + XSLT import overrides

ul.li: provides an override XSL file via dita.xsl.html5 and/or dita.xsl.html5.toc
imports the default transform and overrides specific templates
easier upgrades (you don't edit toolkit core)

p.b: Strategy B: Build-time custom XSL via args.xsl

conbody.p: You supply a single XSL file at build time (parameter-driven). Good for:

ul.li: customer-specific builds that should not live in your core plugin

conbody.p: Risk: unmanaged sprawl (multiple XSLs floating around, unclear precedence, harder upgrades).

ul.li: customization is part of your product pipeline → plug-in
